text.title: cicd-mtls-deployment Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cicd-mtls-deployment as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using long-lived shared client certs (validity 1 year+) for service-to-service mTLS across multiple microservices.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wildcard certs or shared service certs spread across teams. A single key compromise requires mass rotation with no revocation guarantee (OCSP/CRL is unreliable at scale). Use per-service short-lived certs from an internal CA instead.

failure-mode.description: Disabling HSTS preload without verifying every subdomain is reachable via HTTPS first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: HSTS preload with the wrong domain creates a permanently unreachable subdomain for any user whose browser has cached the HSTS policy. Only add preload after auditing every subdomain in the HSTS scope.

failure-mode.description: Pasting private keys into chat sessions or CI/CD environment variable values as plain text for debugging mTLS issues.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Private keys in chat history, CI/CD logs, or environment variable exports are permanently compromised. Use Vault dynamic secrets or CI/CD secret stores (GitHub Secrets, GCP Secret Manager) to inject private material at runtime without exposing it in logs.

failure-mode.description: Configuring mTLS with ssl_verify_client on nginx but not validating $ssl_client_verify in the backend application.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: nginx may be bypassed by internal routing, misconfigured load balancers, or direct-to-backend traffic in Kubernetes. The backend MUST verify the ssl_client_verify header value equals SUCCESS before trusting any client identity headers.
